ip_outcome_note: Verified. The card name is identical to the character's canonical name (Captain Nathaniel Flint from Treasure Planet, 2002). Mushu Report categorizes the card as Storyborn / Villain / Alien / Pirate / Captain, matching Flint's canonical role exactly. Disney Wiki and Treasure Planet Wiki both describe Flint as belonging to an unnamed alien species with a reptilian multi-eyed face — consistent with the card art's alien pirate captain with non-human features. Lorcanaplayer.com and lorcana.gg both confirm this is the Treasure Planet character. This is a Wizards-internal Disney-owned character (not an external crossover IP), so no ## IP callout block is required.

trivia_bullets.text: Nathaniel Flint is the overarching posthumous antagonist of Disney's 2002 animated film Treasure Planet — a sci-fi retelling of Robert Louis Stevenson's Treasure Island. Flint never appears alive in the film; his hoard and reputation drive the entire plot. His Lorcana card is classified as Storyborn, meaning he exists as himself drawn from the source canon. [Disney Wiki: Captain Nathaniel Flint; lorcanaplayer.com]

Flavor text: "Flint's secret trove was never found, but stories have persisted that it remains hidden..." — Narrator. This mirrors the film's premise: Flint's treasure planet is the object of all ambition, yet Flint himself is a ghost whose presence is felt only through legend and the damage left behind. [card frontmatter]

PREDATORY INSTINCT ability: 'You can't play this character unless an opposing character was damaged this turn.' Mechanically rewards aggression — Flint only arrives when blood is already in the water. A 2-cost Steel Rare with 3/3 stats, solid value if the gate condition is met. [Mushu Report: Nathaniel Flint - Notorious Pirate; lorcanaplayer.com]

Illustrated by Edu Francisco. Card type includes Alien — Flint belonged to an unnamed alien species with a reptilian multi-eyed face, fangs, grey skin, yellow eyes, and long brown hair, per Disney Wiki. The Lorcana art depicts glowing red eyes and a monstrous non-human face at a low angle, a stylized interpretation of the same species. [Disney Wiki: Captain Nathaniel Flint; card frontmatter]

related_cards.name: Jim Hawkins (any Lorcana version)
related_cards.note: Protagonist whose entire quest is driven by Flint's legend

related_cards.name: Long John Silver (any Lorcana version)
related_cards.note: Primary antagonist of Treasure Planet; Flint's spiritual successor in the film
